deep_dive.title: Fidelity- and Perception-Aware Local Implicit Attention for Arbitrary-Scale Image Super-Resolution
deep_dive.abstract: Arbitrary-scale image super-resolution (ASISR) aims to reconstruct high-resolution images from low-resolution inputs over a continuous range of upscaling factors. While traditional pixel-regression approaches often produce overly smooth results that lack realistic details, recent diffusion methods can produce sharper and more realistic textures. However, these diffusion techniques frequently introduce the risk of structural hallucinations. To address these issues, we propose Fidelity- and Perception-Aware Local Implicit Attention (FPLIA), a framework that effectively integrates fidelity-oriented features into a diffusion pipeline to produce realistic and faithful reconstructions for ASISR. We introduce a Fidelity and Perception Attention Module (FPAM), which applies both self-attention and cross-attention to fidelity-oriented and perceptual features to enhance representational capacity. To further exploit their complements, we design a Fidelity and Perception Select Module (FPSM) that adaptively selects the most representative features for RGB values prediction. We conduct extensive experiments to validate the effectiveness of these components. Both qualitative and quantitative results show that FPLIA delivers superior perceptual realism while maintaining reconstruction accuracy on standard ASISR benchmarks. The source code is accessible at the following repository: https://github.com/XUSean0118/FPLIA.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Diffusion pipeline with fidelity-aware attention for arbitrary-scale super-resolution
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散模型中结合保真度感知注意力实现任意尺度超分辨率
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 扩散模型中结合保真度感知注意力实现任意尺度超分辨率
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 扩散模型中结合保真度感知注意力实现任意尺度超分辨率
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FPLIA framework integrating fidelity-oriented features into diffusion pipeline for realistic and faithful arbitrary-scale super-resolution.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对任意尺度超分辨率任务，为解决扩散模型易产生结构性幻觉的问题，提出保真度与感知感知的局部隐式注意力（FPLIA）框架。该框架将保真度导向的特征有效融入扩散管线，在保证感知质量的同时维持结构真实度。实验证明FPLIA在任意尺度超分辨率上实现了真实感与保真度的平衡，优于纯回归和纯扩散方法。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models

deep_dive.title: Variance-Tilted Diffusion Models for Diverse Sampling
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion models are typically sampled independently, even when the downstream objective is to obtain a diverse set of candidates. We introduce a variance-weighted batch distribution that favours collections of samples with large empirical spread after a prescribed linear feature map. The target is specified explicitly, and the sampler is derived as the corresponding Doob $h$-transform of independent diffusion dynamics. The resulting correction has a compact form: an interaction term that repels posterior denoised means, together with a curvature term that moves particles to the region of higher feature variance. This yields an interacting-particle sampler with a transparent probabilistic target rather than a heuristic repulsive drift.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Variance-weighted batch distribution for diverse sampling in diffusion models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对扩散模型的方差加权批次分布以实现多样化采样
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对扩散模型的方差加权批次分布以实现多样化采样
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对扩散模型的方差加权批次分布以实现多样化采样
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: We introduce a variance-weighted batch distribution to encourage diverse sampling in diffusion models, derived as a Doob h-transform with an interaction term that repels similar samples. This yields an interacting-particle sampler with a clear probabilistic target, improving diversity over independent sampling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型通常独立采样，即使目标是获得多样化的候选集。本文提出方差加权批次分布，通过一个规定的线性特征图后经验传播较大的样本集合得到奖励。采样器被导出为独立扩散动力学的Doob h变换，修正项包括排斥后验去噪均值的相互作用项和将粒子移动到较高特征方差区域的曲率项。该方法提供了一个概率透明的目标，而非启发式排斥漂移，为扩散模型多样化采样提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 扩散模型通常独立采样，即使目标是获得多样化的候选集。本文提出方差加权批次分布，通过一个规定的线性特征图后经验传播较大的样本集合得到奖励。采样器被导出为独立扩散动力学的Doob h变换，修正项包括排斥后验去噪均值的相互作用项和将粒子移动到较高特征方差区域的曲率项。该方法提供了一个概率透明的目标，而非启发式排斥漂移，为扩散模型多样化采样提供了新方案。

deep_dive.title: Lighting-Consistent Object Transfer Across Radiance Fields
deep_dive.abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) is widely used to capture and render real scenes. Compositing objects from one capture into another has applications in many domains, such as VFX, architecture and interior design, or marketing. However, extracting an object from a source scene and naively pasting it into a target scene will fail to produce realistic results due to the different lighting conditions between the two scenes. To address this problem, we introduce a diffusion model that harmonizes naively composited images with inconsistent lighting. The model is trained with a heterogeneous dataset of image pairs (inconsistent composite input, consistent output), combining synthetic, generated, and real data. Our complete 3D solution allows a user to extract an object from the source scene and composite it into the target scene. From this, the (inconsistent) views of the target scene with the composite object are rendered. Our diffusion model harmonizes each one of these views, which are finally consolidated in a 3DGS representation with a post-optimization step. Our method provides visually compelling results, making object transfer between 3DGS easy to use and significantly improving quality compared to previous methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Uses diffusion model for lighting-consistent object compositing, enabling realistic image editing across radiance fields
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型实现光照一致的物体合成，支持跨辐射场的逼真图像编辑
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型实现光照一致的物体合成，支持跨辐射场的逼真图像编辑
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用扩散模型实现光照一致的物体合成，支持跨辐射场的逼真图像编辑
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes a 3D object compositing pipeline that uses a diffusion model to correct lighting inconsistencies when transferring objects between scenes, trained on a heterogeneous dataset of composite-to-consistent image pairs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对3D高斯泼溅中物体合成时光照不一致的问题，本文提出一种基于扩散模型的图像和谐方法。通过合成、生成和真实数据构建异质训练集，训练扩散模型将光照不一致的合成图像转换为光照一致的输出，并结合3DGS提取和重渲染流程，实现跨场景的逼真物体迁移。该方法在视觉效果和定量指标上均优于直接粘贴，为3D场景编辑提供了实用工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对3D高斯泼溅中物体合成时光照不一致的问题，本文提出一种基于扩散模型的图像和谐方法。通过合成、生成和真实数据构建异质训练集，训练扩散模型将光照不一致的合成图像转换为光照一致的输出，并结合3DGS提取和重渲染流程，实现跨场景的逼真物体迁移。该方法在视觉效果和定量指标上均优于直接粘贴，为3D场景编辑提供了实用工具。

deep_dive.title: NullFlow: One-Step Generative Reconstruction
deep_dive.abstract: We propose NullFlow, a principled framework for one-step generative image reconstruction. Our key idea is to confine the generative flow to a measurement-consistent subspace. Because the flow never leaves this subspace, NullFlow needs no separate data-fidelity corrections, unlike existing solvers. NullFlow samples in a single network evaluation by learning the flow's average velocity, avoiding the step-by-step integration of traditional flow matching methods. We prove that the average velocity of this constrained flow yields a training objective whose global minimizer is a one-step posterior sampler. We show on image inpainting that NullFlow matches state-of-the-art diffusion solvers while cutting inference from hundreds of network evaluations to one.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Generative image reconstruction via flow matching for inpainting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用流匹配进行图像修复的生成式重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用流匹配进行图像修复的生成式重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用流匹配进行图像修复的生成式重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: NullFlow proposes a one-step generative reconstruction framework by constraining the flow to a measurement-consistent subspace, eliminating data-fidelity corrections. It achieves state-of-the-art inpainting quality with a single network evaluation, reducing inference from hundreds of steps to one.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有生成式图像重建方法常需数百次网络评估，推断缓慢。本文提出NullFlow框架，将生成流约束在测量一致子空间内，无需额外数据保真校正，通过学习流的平均速度实现单步采样。在图像修复任务上，NullFlow达到与最先进扩散求解器相当的修复质量，而推断时间从数百步降至一步，极大提升了实用性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有生成式图像重建方法常需数百次网络评估，推断缓慢。本文提出NullFlow框架，将生成流约束在测量一致子空间内，无需额外数据保真校正，通过学习流的平均速度实现单步采样。在图像修复任务上，NullFlow达到与最先进扩散求解器相当的修复质量，而推断时间从数百步降至一步，极大提升了实用性。

deep_dive.title: Learning Adaptive Dynamical Features via Multi-$τ$ Liquid-Mamba for All-in-one Image Restoration
deep_dive.abstract: Image restoration aims to recover high-quality images from degraded observations. Recent Mamba-based image restoration models have demonstrated strong potential in modeling long-range dependencies with linear complexity. However, most existing designs still rely on a single state-evolution timescale, which limits their adaptability to spatially heterogeneous and task-dependent degradation patterns in all-in-one image restoration. In this paper, we propose Multi-$τ$ Liquid-Mamba, an adaptive state space module that introduces input-conditioned multi-timescale liquid discretization into selective state space modeling. Instead of changing the overall selective scan pipeline, the proposed module modulates the effective discretization steps of multiple dynamical branches and adaptively fuses their responses according to degradation-aware gating weights. This design allows the model to capture both fast-varying local details and slowly evolving global structures while preserving the linear scaling property of Mamba with respect to sequence length. Importantly, Multi-$τ$ Liquid-Mamba modulates the effective transition dynamics while preserving the original selective parameterization and hardware-efficient selective scan mechanism, making it a plug-and-play module that can be seamlessly integrated into existing Mamba-based architectures. Built upon this framework, we develop a Multi-$τ$ Liquid-Mamba Image Restoration Network (MLMIR) for all-in-one image restoration. Extensive experiments on a wide range of restoration benchmarks demonstrate that MLMIR consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance in all-in-one image restoration while remaining highly competitive in task-aligned restoration settings.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Multi-timescale Mamba for all-in-one image restoration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多时间尺度Mamba用于全能图像修复
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多时间尺度Mamba用于全能图像修复
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多时间尺度Mamba用于全能图像修复
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Multi-τ Liquid-Mamba, an adaptive state space module with multi-timescale discretization for all-in-one image restoration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有Mamba图像修复模型仅使用单一状态演化时间尺度而难以适应异质退化的问题，提出多时间尺度液态离散状态空间模块，增强对空间异质和任务依赖退化的适应能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有Mamba图像修复模型仅使用单一状态演化时间尺度而难以适应异质退化的问题，提出多时间尺度液态离散状态空间模块，增强对空间异质和任务依赖退化的适应能力。

deep_dive.title: Controllable Texture Tiling with Transformed RoPE-Enhanced Diffusion Models
deep_dive.abstract: Realistic integration of user-specified textures into scene images is a fundamental task in computer graphics and image editing. While existing material transfer and reference-guided inpainting methods can edit surface appearances, they often fail to address the specific requirements of texture tiling. This task necessitates precisely repeating a reference pattern according to user-defined parameters such as frequency, orientation, and scale. Furthermore, current generative approaches often struggle to maintain the structural fidelity of the reference texture, limited by either destructive pixel-level resampling or the lack of fine-grained spatial information in semantic image encoders, and they frequently fail to preserve the coherent lighting and geometry of the original scene. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for controllable and high-fidelity texture tiling based on Diffusion Transformers. Our approach introduces two key technical innovations to decouple spatial manipulation from content generation. First, we propose a Coordinate-Transformed Rotary Embedding mechanism. By applying 2D affine transformations directly to the relative positional embeddings between the target latent and the image condition, we achieve precise control over tiling patterns without explicit pixel warping, thereby utilizing the full information of the reference condition without degradation. Second, a Disjoint Attention Mask is employed to shield reference features from semantic leakage. This preserves structural integrity while seamlessly blending the synthesized texture with the scene's original lighting and geometry. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in both control accuracy and texture fidelity.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Controllable texture tiling using diffusion models for precise pattern placement in image editing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型实现可控纹理贴图，用于图像编辑中的精确图案放置
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型实现可控纹理贴图，用于图像编辑中的精确图案放置
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用扩散模型实现可控纹理贴图，用于图像编辑中的精确图案放置
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This work presents a diffusion-based method for texture tiling in images that preserves pattern structure and lighting by leveraging transformed RoPE-enhanced attention, enabling user-controlled frequency, orientation, and scale.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对图像编辑中纹理贴图时结构变形和光照不一致等问题，本文提出一种基于变换后RoPE增强的扩散模型方法，能够根据用户指定的频率、方向和尺度参数精确铺贴纹理。该方法改进了注意力机制以保留纹理细节和全局光照一致性，在多个场景上实现了高保真可控纹理合成，优于现有材料迁移和修复方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对图像编辑中纹理贴图时结构变形和光照不一致等问题，本文提出一种基于变换后RoPE增强的扩散模型方法，能够根据用户指定的频率、方向和尺度参数精确铺贴纹理。该方法改进了注意力机制以保留纹理细节和全局光照一致性，在多个场景上实现了高保真可控纹理合成，优于现有材料迁移和修复方法。

deep_dive.title: Vera: A Layered Diffusion Model for Content-Preserving Video Editing
deep_dive.abstract: Video diffusion models have enabled remarkable progress in video generation and editing. However, content preservation remains a core challenge: existing methods regenerate every pixel and often alter elements that should remain unchanged, such as characters or background scenes. We introduce Vera, a layered diffusion framework for content-preserving video editing. Instead of regenerating the entire video, Vera generates an edit layer along with an alpha matte for compositing with the source video, separating creative editing from content preservation by design. To encourage coherent composition with the source video, we extend the text-to-video DiT into a Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) architecture, with separate DiTs for each layer that interact through joint self-attention. To support the training of Vera, we further construct a high-quality layered dataset with accurate alpha mattes, diverse scenes and dynamics, and visual effects. Across our quantitative benchmark and human preference study, Vera outperforms leading open-source video editing models in content preservation while remaining competitive in edit quality, using 486K frames of layered training data.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Content-preserving video editing using a layered diffusion framework
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用分层扩散框架进行保持内容的视频编辑
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用分层扩散框架进行保持内容的视频编辑
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用分层扩散框架进行保持内容的视频编辑
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Vera, a layered diffusion model for video editing that generates an edit layer and alpha matte to preserve source content, using a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视频编辑中内容保持难题，提出Vera分层扩散框架，通过生成编辑图层与透明度蒙版实现分离式编辑，避免全像素重生成导致的内容改变。扩展DiT为混合Transformer结构，保持源视频一致性。实验表明该框架能有效保持身份与背景，同时实现灵活编辑，为视频编辑设立了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对视频编辑中内容保持难题，提出Vera分层扩散框架，通过生成编辑图层与透明度蒙版实现分离式编辑，避免全像素重生成导致的内容改变。扩展DiT为混合Transformer结构，保持源视频一致性。实验表明该框架能有效保持身份与背景，同时实现灵活编辑，为视频编辑设立了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Enlight: Fast Low-Light Image Enhancement via Multi-Objective Optimization and Shadow-Aware Refinement
quick_skim.abstract: We present ENLIGHT, a fast and training free framework for low-light image enhancement based on direct optimization of a perceptual objective. Unlike deep learning approaches that require large scale training data and supervision, ENLIGHT operates in a zero-shot manner by optimizing image quality at inference time. The method employs a two stage global to local optimization strategy. In the first stage, ENLIGHT performs global illumination adjustment to improve visibility while maintaining structural consistency and avoiding excessive noise enhancement. In the second stage, a shadow aware refinement selectively improves low-intensity regions through masked local optimization, enhancing visibility without overexposure. To balance quality and efficiency, we introduce two modes: Fast, which uses a multi-objective formulation combining entropy, gradient preservation, and noise regularization, and Ultrafast, which reduces computational cost via a lightweight approximation of the same objective. The framework is optimizer agnostic and supports both evolutionary and lightweight local search methods. Experiments on BAID, Backlit300, LIME, MEF, NPE, and DICM demonstrate that ENLIGHT achieves competitive perceptual quality (MUSIQ, NIQE, BRISQUE) with significantly lower inference time. Qualitative results further show improved contrast, preserved structural details, and controlled noise amplification, making ENLIGHT a practical and interpretable alternative to learning based methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Zero-shot low-light image enhancement via multi-objective optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过多目标优化的零样本低光图像增强
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过多目标优化的零样本低光图像增强
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过多目标优化的零样本低光图像增强
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ENLIGHT, a training-free framework for low-light image enhancement using global-to-local optimization and shadow-aware refinement.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出零样本低光图像增强框架ENLIGHT，通过全局照明调整和阴影感知局部优化两步策略，在无需训练数据的情况下提升可见性并抑制噪声，实现快速高质量的增强。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出零样本低光图像增强框架ENLIGHT，通过全局照明调整和阴影感知局部优化两步策略，在无需训练数据的情况下提升可见性并抑制噪声，实现快速高质量的增强。

quick_skim.title: Multimodal Image Colorization: Quantifying the Impact of Text-Conditioned Guidance on Grayscale-to-Color Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Grayscale images are commonly found in historical photography restoration, medical imaging, and artistic media. However, automatically applying color to these images remains a significant challenge in computer vision because many plausible colorizations can correspond to the same grayscale input.   In this work, we quantify the effect of text conditioning on pixel-level and perceptual metrics for grayscale-to-color image models. Specifically, we compare two architectures, a U-Net and Stable Diffusion 1.5, each tested with and without CLIP text conditioning while holding all other variables constant. Our results show that text conditioning improves PSNR by 5.6%, SSIM by 1.2%, and colorfulness by 36.6%, while reducing LPIPS by 7.6% in the U-Net tier. In the Stable Diffusion tier, text conditioning improves PSNR by 5.8%, SSIM by 1.5%, and colorfulness by 0.6%, while reducing LPIPS by 11.3%. These results indicate that text conditioning provides consistent, measurable improvements to colorization quality across both architecture scales.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Quantifies text guidance impact on colorization using Stable Diffusion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 量化文本引导对使用稳定扩散进行上色的影响
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 量化文本引导对使用稳定扩散进行上色的影响
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 量化文本引导对使用稳定扩散进行上色的影响
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The study quantifies text-conditioned guidance in grayscale-to-color translation, showing that CLIP text conditioning improves colorfulness and perceptual quality in both U-Net and Stable Diffusion models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 灰度图像上色是多模态问题，传统方法缺乏对文本条件影响的系统量化。本文通过控制实验比较U-Net与Stable Diffusion 1.5在有无CLIP文本条件下色化性能，发现文本条件在下将PSNR提升5.6%、SSIM提升1.2%、色彩丰富度提升36.6%，揭示了文本引导对上色感知质量的显著增益，为可控图像编辑提供了定量依据。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 灰度图像上色是多模态问题，传统方法缺乏对文本条件影响的系统量化。本文通过控制实验比较U-Net与Stable Diffusion 1.5在有无CLIP文本条件下色化性能，发现文本条件在下将PSNR提升5.6%、SSIM提升1.2%、色彩丰富度提升36.6%，揭示了文本引导对上色感知质量的显著增益，为可控图像编辑提供了定量依据。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models

quick_skim.title: Text-to-Image Generative AI for Modeling and Simulation: Methods, Opportunities, and Applications
quick_skim.abstract: Text-to-image generation is a form of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) that converts textual descriptions into images. Most applications of GenAI in modeling and simulation (M&S) have focused on large language models for documentation, coding, or explanation. By contrast, the potential of image generation remains largely unexplored. This tutorial introduces text-to-image generation to the M&S community and details how it can support several M&S tasks, including communicating conceptual models, visualizing simulation outcomes, generating educational materials, and interfacing heterogeneous models in multi-scale simulations. The tutorial combines conceptual guidance with practical workflows, explaining how modern image generators operate, how prompts and simulation outputs can be translated into visual scenes, and how practitioners can integrate these tools into reproducible local pipelines. By focusing on transferable principles rather than specific tools, the tutorial equips M&S practitioners with the knowledge needed to evaluate, adopt, and adapt text-to-image generation in their simulation workflows.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Tutorial on applying text-to-image generative models for modeling and simulation tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 关于将文本到图像生成模型应用于建模与仿真任务的教程
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 关于将文本到图像生成模型应用于建模与仿真任务的教程
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 关于将文本到图像生成模型应用于建模与仿真任务的教程
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This tutorial introduces text-to-image generative AI to the modeling and simulation community, covering methods like diffusion models and demonstrating applications such as conceptual model visualization and simulation output rendering.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文向建模与仿真社区介绍文本到图像生成式AI，包括扩散模型等方法，并通过实际工作流程展示其在概念模型可视化、仿真结果渲染、教育资料生成等任务中的应用。该教程为M&S领域利用生成式图像AI提供了概念指导和实践框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文向建模与仿真社区介绍文本到图像生成式AI，包括扩散模型等方法，并通过实际工作流程展示其在概念模型可视化、仿真结果渲染、教育资料生成等任务中的应用。该教程为M&S领域利用生成式图像AI提供了概念指导和实践框架。

quick_skim.title: Causal Variational Deep Embedding: A Family of Interventional Generators for Confounded Images
quick_skim.abstract: Deep generative models reproduce the observational distribution of their training data, inheriting any spurious associations it contains. A common source is an unobserved confounder that shapes both an attribute the user wants to control at sampling time and an attribute expected to vary in response. Existing causal generative approaches resolve the resulting ambiguity by imposing structural assumptions strong enough to single out one interventional distribution; in image domains, such assumptions are rarely warranted, and the data is generally consistent with a set of distinct causal mechanisms -- a feasible region of interventional distributions. We propose CauVaDE (Causal Variational Deep Embedding), built on a canonical augmented SCM in which the unobserved confounder collapses, without loss of generality, into a discrete latent cluster of bounded support while continuous variation is absorbed into independent noises. We prove that this canonical class is dense, in both observational and interventional Wasserstein distance, in the class of augmented SCMs compatible with a given causal diagram, and instantiate it as a mixture variational autoencoder whose cluster variable plays the role of the canonical confounder. An entropy regularizer with weight $γ$ on the cluster posterior then traces a family of candidate causal effects that fit the observational data to comparable likelihood while spanning the feasible region. Experiments on image data benchmarks show that CauVaDE produces diverse interventional samples and improves FID against an unconfounded reference.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Causal generative model for controllable image generation without confounders
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无混淆因子的因果生成模型用于可控图像生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无混淆因子的因果生成模型用于可控图像生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无混淆因子的因果生成模型用于可控图像生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CauVaDE augments deep generative models with causal inference to enable controllable image generation by learning interventional distributions, addressing confounding in real-world images.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 深度生成模型常继承训练数据中的虚假关联，受隐藏混淆因子影响。本文提出CauVaDE，基于因果图扩展变分自编码器，学习一组可行的干预分布，允许用户在采样时控制属性而不引入混淆偏差，为图像生成提供因果可控性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 深度生成模型常继承训练数据中的虚假关联，受隐藏混淆因子影响。本文提出CauVaDE，基于因果图扩展变分自编码器，学习一组可行的干预分布，允许用户在采样时控制属性而不引入混淆偏差，为图像生成提供因果可控性。

quick_skim.title: ShuffleFlow: Scalable Posterior Inference for Bayesian Inverse Imaging
quick_skim.abstract: Variational inference (VI) is a powerful method for principled posterior inference for scientific inverse imaging. VI learns the posterior distribution, often with a flow-based network, which can cheaply generate posterior samples upon optimization, and can flexibly incorporate score-based or classic priors. However, its application to large-scale image reconstruction is severely hindered by the poor scalability of the flow-based networks. In this work, we introduce ShuffleFlow, a scalable VI framework to address this challenge. Our method breaks down the problem into three parts: a pixel-unshuffling-based image coordinate sampler, a neural field as feature encoder, and a conditional normalizing flow (CNF) as posterior estimator. Specifically, our framework partitions an image into a stack of sub-images with pixel-unshuffling and uses a shared CNF to model the joint distribution of the sub-image stack. We condition the CNF on the output of a neural field, which embeds feature vectors corresponding to pixel-unshuffling sample locations to capture spatial structures, and share the flow's latent variable across the channels to model their correlations. We demonstrate our method's effectiveness and efficiency on both linear and nonlinear imaging inverse problems, and show its ability to more rapidly generate a high-sample-count posterior than diffusion samplers.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Scalable variational inference for inverse imaging using flow-based generative models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于流生成模型的可扩展变分推断用于逆成像
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于流生成模型的可扩展变分推断用于逆成像
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于流生成模型的可扩展变分推断用于逆成像
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper addresses the scalability issue of flow-based variational inference for large-scale image reconstruction by introducing ShuffleFlow, which decomposes the problem with pixel-unshuffling, neural fields, and conditional normalizing flows, enabling efficient posterior sampling for scientific inverse imaging.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对基于流的变分推断在大尺度图像重建中可扩展性差的问题，本文提出ShuffleFlow框架，通过像素反混洗坐标采样、神经场特征编码和条件归一化流后验估计，实现高效可扩展的后验推断。该方法能灵活结合得分先验或经典先验，在逆成像任务中表现优越，为大规模科学图像重建提供了实用工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对基于流的变分推断在大尺度图像重建中可扩展性差的问题，本文提出ShuffleFlow框架，通过像素反混洗坐标采样、神经场特征编码和条件归一化流后验估计，实现高效可扩展的后验推断。该方法能灵活结合得分先验或经典先验，在逆成像任务中表现优越，为大规模科学图像重建提供了实用工具。

quick_skim.title: Encoder-Decoder Manifold Alignment for Idempotent Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Recently, several learning paradigms have been introduced to enforce idempotency in generative models. The goal is to ensure that repeated application of a model leaves samples unchanged once they lie on the target data manifold. In practice, however, many of these approaches fail to achieve exact fixed points, leading to instability and drift under repeated application. In this work, we argue that a key reason for this failure is a geometric mismatch between the manifolds learned by the encoder and decoder. The encoder projects inputs onto one latent manifold, while the decoder implicitly learns to reconstruct data from a different manifold. This discrepancy prevents the model from learning truly idempotent mappings. To address this issue, we propose a new training framework that explicitly closes this gap by forcing the encoder and decoder to learn consistent representations of the same underlying data manifold. By aligning the geometry of these components, our method encourages stable projections. Empirically, we show that our approach achieves significantly lower idempotency error and consistently regenerates identical outputs under repeated application, compared to existing methods. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework on both image generation and image editing tasks. Finally, we show that enforcing idempotency in this manner improves identity preservation and information stability, leading to more realistic and controllable generative editing models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes encoder-decoder manifold alignment for idempotent generative models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出编码器-解码器流形对齐以实现等幂生成模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出编码器-解码器流形对齐以实现等幂生成模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出编码器-解码器流形对齐以实现等幂生成模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Addresses instability in generative models under repeated application by aligning encoder and decoder manifolds for true idempotency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对生成模型在重复应用下不稳定和漂移的问题，提出通过对齐编码器与解码器的流形来实现真正的等幂映射，从而提升生成模型的固定点收敛性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对生成模型在重复应用下不稳定和漂移的问题，提出通过对齐编码器与解码器的流形来实现真正的等幂映射，从而提升生成模型的固定点收敛性。

quick_skim.title: Physically-guided Image Generation for Multi-Projection Mapping
quick_skim.abstract: Projection Mapping (PM) enables seamless superimposition of digital content onto real-world 3D objects, serving as a fundamental technique for immersive visualization, digital twins, and interactive art. Although text-to-image diffusion models have greatly facilitated customized content creation, directly integrating them into practical PM pipelines remains challenging due to the mismatch between idealized 2D generation and physical constraints. To bridge this gap, this paper formalizes two application-level generative paradigms: the cooperative paradigm (harmonizing generated semantics with physical attributes) and the adversarial paradigm (eliminating surface interference via radiometric compensation). Based on this, we propose ConPhyG, a unified controllable physically-guided generative multi-projection mapping framework that enables creators to interactively adjust physical constraints and flexibly switch generative paradigms. In cooperative mode, multi-dimensional physical priors (per-pixel gamut, depth, and edges) are injected into the diffusion process. In adversarial mode, the framework releases the generative potential and applies bounded numerical optimization for multi-projector radiometric compensation. It allows users to dynamically switch constraints to balance artistic freedom with physical feasibility. Furthermore, we extend ConPhyG to 360-degree multi-view consistent PM using a sequential generation strategy. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations on a real-world four-projector setup demonstrate that ConPhyG significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in geometric alignment, gamut utilization, and semantic fidelity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Applies diffusion models to physically-guided image generation for projection mapping
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将扩散模型应用于投影映射的物理引导图像生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将扩散模型应用于投影映射的物理引导图像生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将扩散模型应用于投影映射的物理引导图像生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ConPhyG bridges diffusion models and projection mapping by formalizing cooperative and adversarial generation paradigms with physical constraints, enabling controllable content creation for real-world applications.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 投影映射需要生成符合物理约束的图像，但现有扩散模型未能直接适应。本文提出ConPhyG，通过协同和对抗两种生成范式，融入物理引导，实现可控的图像生成，弥合了理想生成与物理现实的差距，拓展了扩散模型的应用场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 投影映射需要生成符合物理约束的图像，但现有扩散模型未能直接适应。本文提出ConPhyG，通过协同和对抗两种生成范式，融入物理引导，实现可控的图像生成，弥合了理想生成与物理现实的差距，拓展了扩散模型的应用场景。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索图像修复、超分辨率与生成扩散模型领域的代表性论文，涵盖基础模型、算法改进、训练策略、评测与应用, especially work that connects or combines: I; Generation Models; Image Restoration & Super Resolution; Diffusion Models; Image Editing Models; Generative Models; Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models.

quick_skim.title: Optimising Inpainting Data with Delaunay Averages
quick_skim.abstract: Inpainting-based image compression usually stores an optimised subset of all pixel locations and their colour values. In the decoding phase, the missing data are approximated via inpainting. Since the reconstruction quality depends critically on the selection of the stored data, we introduce a novel feature type: We store the vertex locations of a Delaunay triangulation together with the average colour values inside all triangles. We show that combining this feature type with homogeneous diffusion inpainting creates an elegant mathematical formulation with a positive definite linear system of equations. Even a simple solver such as the conjugate gradient method allows the handling of large images. To make our Delaunay averages maximally adaptive to the image, we develop an efficient data optimisation strategy specifically tailored to them. It incorporates ideas successfully used in the stippling literature. Experiments show that our approach outperforms the popular inpainting with optimised colour values by a large margin. Last but not least, we discover a favourable scaling behaviour: Doubling the image resolution allows us to halve the percentage of stored data while maintaining the quality level. This is attractive for compressing modern high-resolution images, where even data densities below 1 % yield appealing reconstructions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Inpainting-based compression using Delaunay averages and diffusion inpainting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于德劳内平均值和扩散修复的压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于德劳内平均值和扩散修复的压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于德劳内平均值和扩散修复的压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a novel feature type for inpainting-based compression storing Delaunay triangle vertices and average colors, enabling efficient reconstruction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对图像压缩中修复数据选择问题，提出存储德劳内三角剖分顶点与三角形内平均颜色值，结合齐次扩散修复实现高质量重建，并开发自适应选择优化策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对图像压缩中修复数据选择问题，提出存储德劳内三角剖分顶点与三角形内平均颜色值，结合齐次扩散修复实现高质量重建，并开发自适应选择优化策略。

quick_skim.title: Training-Free Semantic Correction for Autoregressive Visual Models
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive visual models (AVMs) based on next-scale prediction have emerged as a prominent paradigm for image and video synthesis. However, decomposing the generation process into discrete scales with varying granularities in AVM makes semantic errors difficult to identify and correct, thereby undermining the quality of the final output. Prior efforts to enhance AVM can be categorized into training-based and training-free approaches. Although training-based efforts to enhance AVM generation quality come at substantial computational cost, existing training-free methods neglect intermediate generation states, leaving semantic errors undiagnosed and allowing them to accumulate into the final output. In this paper, we focus on training-free paradigms and propose Gazer, a framework that integrates multimodal large language model feedback into the AVM sampling loop for in-generation semantic correction. Concretely, Gazer operates via two cooperating stages: the Reflective Diagnosis stage diagnoses semantic errors from intermediate states, while the Semantic Correction stage rewinds and rectifies the generation trajectory to realign with the target prompt. Experiments on compositional image and video benchmarks demonstrate that Gazer improves semantic alignment and compositional accuracy across multiple AVMs without additional training.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Training-free semantic correction for autoregressive visual generation models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对自回归视觉生成模型的免训练语义校正
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对自回归视觉生成模型的免训练语义校正
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对自回归视觉生成模型的免训练语义校正
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a training-free method to correct semantic errors in intermediate states of autoregressive visual models for better image synthesis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自回归视觉模型在多尺度生成中语义错误积累问题，提出免训练的语义校正方法，通过诊断并修正中间状态提升最终输出质量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对自回归视觉模型在多尺度生成中语义错误积累问题，提出免训练的语义校正方法，通过诊断并修正中间状态提升最终输出质量。

quick_skim.title: Robust Diffusion Models via Divergence-Induced Weighted Denoising
quick_skim.abstract: We show that replacing the standard MSE denoising loss in diffusion models with a nonlinear transformation induced by an f-divergence yields a simple robust training surrogate that empirically improves performance under data contamination, with small additional computational overhead. The theoretical foundation rests on a local divergence construction: under the Gaussian reverse-kernel structure of DDPM, each per-step likelihood ratio follows a lognormal distribution parameterized by a scalar mismatch, so the conditional f-divergence at each step reduces to a one-dimensional function of the denoising error. Summing these local divergences yields a training objective that unifies diffusion training as divergence induced weighted denoising, where the derivative of the induced divergence acts as a residual-space influence weight that controls the contribution of each sample. Bounded-influence divergences (Hellinger, negative exponential) suppress large error samples, with Hellinger yielding an explicit exponential weight, connecting the framework to robust M-estimation. Empirically, on CIFAR-10 under 30% contamination, NED reduces FID from 93.0 (KL) to 77.5, while also outperforming standard robust losses such as Huber and clipped MSE.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: robust diffusion training via divergence-induced weighted denoising
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过散度诱导加权去噪实现鲁棒扩散训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过散度诱导加权去噪实现鲁棒扩散训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过散度诱导加权去噪实现鲁棒扩散训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Replaces MSE loss in diffusion models with f-divergence-based objective for robust training under data contamination.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对扩散模型训练中对数据污染敏感的问题，提出用f散度诱导的非线性变换替代MSE损失，实现鲁棒的加权去噪训练。理论分析表明每步似然比服从对数正态分布，从而将f散度简化为去噪误差的一维函数。实验显示该方法在小额计算开销下提高了模型对数据污染的鲁棒性。该工作为扩散模型训练提供了一种抗污染的策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对扩散模型训练中对数据污染敏感的问题，提出用f散度诱导的非线性变换替代MSE损失，实现鲁棒的加权去噪训练。理论分析表明每步似然比服从对数正态分布，从而将f散度简化为去噪误差的一维函数。实验显示该方法在小额计算开销下提高了模型对数据污染的鲁棒性。该工作为扩散模型训练提供了一种抗污染的策略。

quick_skim.title: Nonparametric Deconvolution and Denoising using Simulation Based Inference
quick_skim.abstract: Latent signals are often obscured by measurement noise, yet encode the underlying laws and dynamics of complex systems; learning both the signals and their distributions remains a central challenge in scientific inference. The noise is often non-negligible, and the likelihoods for expressive generative models are often intractable. We utilize a convolutional maximum mean discrepancy (convMMD) loss and propose a likelihood-free framework for nonparametric density deconvolution and empirical Bayes denoising under additive measurement error. Our method learns a latent generative model by matching the observed data distribution to the noise-convolved model distribution. This yields a differentiable, simulation-based objective for multivariate homoscedastic or heteroscedastic noise, compatible with expressive sieve classes such as Gaussian mixtures and normalizing flows. The learned density then serves as an empirical prior for posterior denoising of individual latent values. Theoretically, we extend convMMD from parametric to nonparametric estimation, proving finite-sample bounds for empirical sieve minimizers and $L_2$ convergence rates under Sobolev smoothness. These rates recover the classical inverse-problem dependence: polynomial for ordinary-smooth and logarithmic for super-smooth noises. Our method provides a practical, theoretically grounded approach to deconvolution and denoising under generative latent distribution models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a simulation-based framework for nonparametric deconvolution and denoising using generative models and convMMD loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对测量噪声下潜在信号恢复与分布估计难题，提出基于卷积最大均值差异损失的免似然框架。通过匹配观测分布与噪声卷积模型分布，学习潜在生成模型，实现多元同方差加性噪声下的非参数反卷积和贝叶斯去噪。该方法在数理统计与信号处理中具有广泛适用性，为图像去噪等任务提供了生成式的解决办法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对测量噪声下潜在信号恢复与分布估计难题，提出基于卷积最大均值差异损失的免似然框架。通过匹配观测分布与噪声卷积模型分布，学习潜在生成模型，实现多元同方差加性噪声下的非参数反卷积和贝叶斯去噪。该方法在数理统计与信号处理中具有广泛适用性，为图像去噪等任务提供了生成式的解决办法。
